habitat: Montane and hill forests; densely vegetated wet/damp areas near streams and gullies
story: Meet the Chestnut Headed Tesia (Cettia castaneocoronata), a distinctive bird from the Cettiidae family. Preferring habitats such as Montane and hill forests; densely vegetated wet/damp areas near streams and gullies, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Least Concern (LC), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Chestnut Headed Tesia serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Chestnut Headed Tesia enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
